reasoning: The 5-year TIPS yield has just experienced a sharp short-term rally, moving from 1.27% on November 26 to 1.38% on December 5, as markets priced out a December rate cut following signals from Fed Chair Powell. The current value of 1.38% is the highest since early August 2025 and sits above the recent 30-day moving trend (projected around 1.37%). Because we are starting at a local maximum and the December Fed pause is now largely priced into the market, the short-term probability of a further breakout above 1.38% by December 14 is slightly less than 50% (around 44-46%, aligning with the trend-residual analysis). Moving further out, predicting the yield relies on longer-term macro conditions (inflation cooling vs. resilient growth and debt pressures). Therefore, for the January and March resolution dates, the estimate reverts back toward a pure random walk baseline (approx 50%).
